content_en:
e Impure water is purified by it changing its characteristics naturally or by cleaning it or by adding enough pure water to it that the alteration is removed.

© If a Muslim is in doubt about whether some water is impure or pure, he acts on the basis of what was known for certainty: the general ruling for anything is that of purity [in other words, something is considered pure unless there is clear evidence demonstrating that it has been made impure].

e If one cannot differentiate liquid that is pure from that which is not, one leaves them both and performs tayammum,

e Ifa person is confused about garments, not knowing which is impure or forbidden from that which is pure, he bases his act on what he knows for certain and prays one prayer only [in that garment; in other words, he uses the garment he believes to be pure and prays once; he does not have to pray, change his clothing and then pray another prayer just to make sure that he prayed in a garment that was not impure or forbidden].

The word al-aaniyah (43!) is the plural of inaa (cil). Lexically, it is any container or utensil used for food or drink. The legal definition of the word is the same.

With respect to the composition of utensils, they can be divided into the following categories: (1) utensils made of gold or silver; (2) silver plated utensils; (3) gold plated utensils; (4) expensive utensils due to their material or way of being made; (5) leather utensils; (6) utensils made out of bone; and (7) any other type of utensils, such as those made of pottery or wood.

Every container or utensil made ofa pure substance, costly or not costly, is permissible to be owned and used except for that made from gold or silver or plated with either of them. Hudhaifah narrated that the Prophet (peace be upon him) said,

ioe BUSEY; Sadly SUIT BLAS Y ce BS, GU 3 ad Wh

“Do not drink from a_ gold or silver container and do not eat from such plates for they are for them [the disbelievers] in this world and for us [the believers] in the Hereafter.” (Recorded by al-Bukhari and Muslim.)

What is forbidden to use is also forbidden to possess in a form that is to be used, like possessing musical instruments which is also forbidden.

Note that the above prohibition applies to both men and women due to the generality of the text.

Nothing is to be considered impure simply based on conjecture until one is certain that it is impure. That is, the basic or general ruling is that of purity.

This includes the utensils of the People of the Book [Jews and Christians] and the utensils of the polytheists. The ruling concerning these utensils is that they are permissible for use as long as one is not certain that they are impure. This is because the general ruling is that of purity.

e The clothing of non-Muslims is considered pure as long as one is not certain that they contain some impurity.

e The skin of a dead animal from the types of animals that one is normally allowed to eat is purified by tanning.

e What is cut off from a live animal [meaning, flesh or bones] is considered impure like carrion. However, the wool, feathers, hairs and fur are considered pure even if taken from a live animal.

e It is recommended (sunnah) to cover the containers and drinking utensils and to close the mouths of waterskins. The Prophet (peace be upon him) said,

o oe ter i ote ~ ne Aer ee ° ab pal Sly GeU] “Ade y all Gal S31) Glee A o,- , fogo a lage dele owt yl “Tie and close the mouths of the water containers and mention the name of Allah [over them]. Cover your containers, even if just with a piece of wood, and mention

the name of Allah [over them].” (Recorded by al-Bukhari and Muslim.)

Lexically, impurities or al-najaasah (4\on3!) means something filthy. Something becomes impure when it is tarnished by something filthy.

In the terminology of Islamic law, impurity refers to a specific amount of particular substances, such as urine, blood and alcohol, whose presence prohibits one from being able to perform the prayer.

There are two types of impurities. One is something that is impure due to its essence and the second is impure with respect to its ruling. The first category includes those articles that are impure in and of themselves such that they cannot be made pure, such as dogs and pigs. The second category covers those things that are impure due to an impure substance coming upon something otherwise pure.

Impurities can be divided into three categories: (1) those substances concerning which there is agreement that they are impure; (2) those substances concerning which there is a disagreement that they are impure; and (3) those substances that are pardoned or overlooked by Islamic law.

(1) Carrion of all land animals [except for locusts]. Dead sea animals are considered pure and permissible to eat.

(2) Blood that has been poured forth, that is, the blood that pours forth from a land animal when it is slaughtered.

(3) Pig flesh [and other parts of the pig].

(7) Al-wadi [an uncommon white substance that flows from the male private part due to a medical condition].

(8) Meat from an animal that one is not allowed to consume.

(9) [Flesh or bones] cut off from a live animal. For example, if one were to cut off the foreleg of a live sheep, that foreleg would be considered impure.

(12) Blood from a prolonged flow from the woman’s private part.

[(13) Al-madhi or prostatic fluid which is a fluid that flows out of the sexual parts due to sexual stimulation.!]

(1) The urine of an animal that one is permitted to eat.

(2) The feces of an animal that one is permitted to eat.

(6) Dead animals that do not have flowing blood, such as ants, cockroaches, fleas and so forth.

(3) Pus from a human or from an animal one is permitted to eat.

' This point was not mentioned in the original text but it is something that is agreed upon—JZ

Impurities are purified [or removed] by washing, moistening, scrubbing or wiping, as follows:

The purification of clothing having impurities: If the impurity has some body or weight to it, it is to be rubbed and scraped off and then washed. Ifthe impurity is wet or moist, then it is to be washed.

The urine of a weaning boy who does not yet eat solid foods needs simply to be moistened.

Impurities on the ground are to be physically removed and then water is poured over any liquid impurity.

Shoes are made pure by rubbing them or walking over pure areas,

Finished or polished items, such as glass, knives, tiles and similar items are cleaned by wiping them.

If a dog licks into a bowl, the bowl is to be washed seven times, one of them being with dirt.

Topic Four: Etiquette and Cleaning Oneself After Relieving Oneself

The word al-istinjaa (¢\~Y\) means to remove the remains of one’s feces or urine with water. The word alistiimaar ()\eo:—~Y|) means to remove the remains of one’s feces or urine with rocks, paper or something of that nature.

It is recommended for the person to enter the bathroom with his left foot first and to say,

or ae, 6 4; y Helly EEN Se all, 5 y5F al ey

“In the name of Allah. I seek refuge in Allah from the male and female devils.”

Upon leaving the bathroom, it is recommended to step out with the right foot first and to say,

ples oS 2 Caf cil al) Mod sige “(I seek] Your forgiveness. All praise be to Allah who removed from me what is harmful and made me healthy.”

It is recommended for the one who is relieving himself to lean on his left leg [meaning the left thigh]. It is also recommended for him to be out of the sight of the people and to conceal himself. For urination, he should choose a place that would prevent his urine from getting on to his clothing.

It is disliked to enter the bathroom with anything containing the name of Allah, unless there is some strong need to do so. One should also avoid raising one’s clothing before getting close to the ground. One should also avoid speaking in the bathroom. He should also avoid touching his private part with his right hand or cleaning it with his right hand.

It is forbidden to face or have one’s back toward the qiblah [the direction in which one prays] while relieving oneself in open lands. If one is in a building, it is permissible, although even then it is best to avoid it.

It is forbidden to urinate or defecate in the roadways, useful areas of shade, under a fruit-producing tree and similar other locations [in other words, any place that will bring harm or discomfort to others].

One should clean oneself with a clean rock, wiping oneself three times, if that totally cleans the person. If that does not clean the person, he should wipe himself more. It is recommended to make it an odd number of times, three, five and so forth.

It is forbidden to clean oneself with bones, dung, food or anything respectable. It is allowed to remove the remains

with water, napkins or paper. To combine both water and rocks is better than to use water alone.

It is obligatory to wash with water the parts of one’s clothing that have impurities on them. If one is not sure exactly where such places are, he is to wash the entire garment. It is from the sunnah for a man to urinate sitting. It is not disliked for him to do so standing given that he can keep himself free from any impurities landing on him.

Toplc Five: The Acts Corresponding to the Nature of Humans (Sunan al-Fitra

In essence, these are the acts that are consistent with the natural and original way that humans were fashioned to be. Hence, they are the acts that a human should fulfill in his life.

(1) Using the toothstick [or toothbrush]: It is recommend to use this at any time, as it is purifying for the mouth and pleasing to the Lord. However, it is even more emphasized while making abilution for prayer, before reading the Quran, upon entering the mosque or house, when rising from one’s sleep and whenever one’s breath has changed its smell.

(2) Shaving the pubic hairs, plucking the underarm hairs, trimming the [finger and toe] nails and washing the finger joints and knuckles.

(3) Trimming the moustache and allowing the beard to grow full and big.

(4) Treating the hair on one’s head properly by oiling and combing it. It is disliked to shave part of the head while leaving the rest unshaved. This is considered a type of mutilation.

(5) Dyeing the gray hairs with henna or katam [a type of herb used in dyeing hair].

(7) Getting circumcised: This is the cutting off of the foreskin of the male organ so that filth and urine cannot build up there. With respect to the female, it is the cutting off of the tip of the clitoris, which looks like a seed. [It is not the same as what is known as “female genital mutilation,” which implies the removal of the entire clitoris and which is forbidden.] It is known to those who specialize in this field. Circumcision is an act of purification and cleanliness. It has many advantages to it. It is a recommended act for males and a noble act for females.

Ablution is the use of purifying water [in washing] the four extremities [and the head] in the manner particularly prescribed by the Law.

The virtues of wudu are indicated in the hadith of the Prophet (peace be upon him) which states,

° ° 4 a0 2° v° ay “ Gow eo ° A , Y of gat Oya See an Beg oh, Se & e477 dor 9G 9 2 5

Srr Gees YU lo; di de Wood of ANE As hey. env rales real

“None of you makes ablution and completes the ablution fully and then says, ‘I bear witness that none is worthy of worship except Allah and that Muhammad is the servant of Allah and His messenger,’ except that opened for him will be the eight gates of Paradise and he enters from any of them he wishes.” (Recorded by Muslim.)

Completing the washing fully of the extremities, without extravagance, entitles one to be among al-ghurr almuhajjaloon on the Day of Resurrection. The Prophet (peace be upon him) said,

+ 2's UT ty Clase I? 3 wal oy 1 ON a ol ° or ae so? va , “ pris aie See! 0 St ¢ Uae. os

“My nation will be called on the Day of Resurrection by the name ghurr muhajjileen [implying that the parts of the body will be glittering or radiant] due to the traces of ablution.
content_id:
Air najis dapat disucikan dengan cara karakteristiknya berubah secara alami, atau dengan membersihkannya, atau dengan menambahkan air bersih yang cukup sehingga perubahan tersebut hilang.

Jika seorang Muslim ragu apakah suatu air itu najis atau suci, maka ia bertindak berdasarkan apa yang diketahui dengan pasti: hukum umum bagi segala sesuatu adalah suci [dengan kata lain, sesuatu dianggap suci kecuali ada bukti jelas yang menunjukkan bahwa benda itu telah menjadi najis].

Jika seseorang tidak dapat membedakan cairan yang suci dari yang tidak, maka ia meninggalkan keduanya dan melakukan tayammum.

Jika seseorang bingung tentang pakaian, tidak tahu mana yang najis atau terlarang dari yang suci, maka ia mendasarkan tindakannya pada apa yang diketahuinya dengan pasti dan hanya melakukan satu kali shalat [dengan pakaian itu].

Kata al-aniyah adalah bentuk jamak dari ina. Secara leksikal, kata ini merujuk pada wadah atau perabot apa pun yang digunakan untuk makanan atau minuman. Definisi hukumnya sama.

Berkenaan dengan komposisi peralatan, peralatan dapat dibagi menjadi kategori-kategori berikut: (1) peralatan yang terbuat dari emas atau perak; (2) peralatan berlapis perak; (3) peralatan berlapis emas; (4) peralatan mahal karena bahan atau cara pembuatannya; (5) peralatan dari kulit; (6) peralatan yang terbuat dari tulang; dan (7) jenis peralatan lainnya, seperti yang terbuat dari tembikar atau kayu.

Setiap wadah atau peralatan yang terbuat dari bahan suci, mahal atau tidak mahal, boleh dimiliki dan digunakan kecuali yang terbuat dari emas atau perak atau berlapis salah satunya. Hudzaifah (ra) meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Janganlah kalian minum dari wadah emas atau perak dan jangan makan dari piring semacam itu karena itu untuk mereka [orang-orang kafir] di dunia ini dan untuk kita [orang-orang mukmin] di akhirat." (Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari dan Muslim.)

Apa yang dilarang untuk digunakan juga dilarang untuk dimiliki dalam bentuk yang akan digunakan, seperti memiliki alat musik yang juga dilarang. Perlu dicatat bahwa larangan di atas berlaku untuk laki-laki maupun perempuan karena keumuman teks tersebut.

Tidak ada yang dianggap najis hanya berdasarkan dugaan semata sampai seseorang yakin bahwa benda itu najis. Artinya, hukum dasar atau umum adalah kesucian.

Ini termasuk peralatan Ahli Kitab [Yahudi dan Kristen] dan peralatan kaum musyrik. Hukum mengenai peralatan ini adalah boleh digunakan selama seseorang tidak yakin bahwa peralatan itu najis. Hal ini karena hukum umum adalah kesucian.

Pakaian orang-orang non-Muslim dianggap suci selama seseorang tidak yakin bahwa pakaian itu mengandung najis. Kulit hewan yang sudah mati dari jenis hewan yang biasanya boleh dimakan disucikan dengan disamak. Apa yang dipotong dari hewan yang masih hidup [yakni, daging atau tulang] dianggap najis seperti bangkai. Namun, wol, bulu, rambut, dan bulu tebal dianggap suci meskipun diambil dari hewan yang masih hidup.

Dianjurkan (sunnah) untuk menutup wadah dan peralatan minum serta menutup mulut kantong air. Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Ikat dan tutuplah mulut wadah-wadah air dan sebutlah nama Allah [atas wadah-wadah itu]. Tutuplah wadah-wadah kalian, meski hanya dengan sepotong kayu, dan sebutlah nama Allah [atas wadah-wadah itu]." (Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari dan Muslim.)

Secara leksikal, najis atau al-najasat berarti sesuatu yang kotor. Sesuatu menjadi najis ketika ternoda oleh sesuatu yang kotor. Dalam terminologi hukum Islam, najis merujuk pada jumlah tertentu dari zat-zat tertentu, seperti urine, darah, dan alkohol, yang kehadirannya melarang seseorang untuk melaksanakan shalat.

Ada dua jenis najis. Yang pertama adalah sesuatu yang najis karena zatnya sendiri dan yang kedua najis berkenaan dengan hukumnya. Kategori pertama mencakup benda-benda yang najis pada dirinya sendiri sehingga tidak dapat disucikan, seperti anjing dan babi. Kategori kedua mencakup hal-hal yang najis karena zat najis menempel pada sesuatu yang sebelumnya suci.

Najis dapat dibagi menjadi tiga kategori: (1) zat-zat yang disepakati kenajisannya; (2) zat-zat yang diperselisihkan kenajisannya; dan (3) zat-zat yang dimaafkan atau diabaikan oleh hukum Islam.

Zat-zat yang disepakati kenajisannya antara lain: (1) Bangkai semua hewan darat [kecuali belalang]. Hewan laut yang mati dianggap suci dan boleh dimakan. (2) Darah yang mengalir deras, yaitu darah yang mengalir dari hewan darat ketika disembelih. (3) Daging babi [dan bagian-bagian lain dari babi]. (7) Al-wadi [zat putih yang tidak umum mengalir dari kemaluan laki-laki karena kondisi medis]. (8) Daging dari hewan yang tidak boleh dikonsumsi. (9) [Daging atau tulang] yang dipotong dari hewan yang masih hidup. (12) Darah dari aliran berkepanjangan dari kemaluan perempuan. (13) Al-madzi atau cairan prostatik yaitu cairan yang keluar dari organ seksual karena rangsangan seksual.

Zat-zat yang diperselisihkan kenajisannya antara lain: (1) Air kencing hewan yang boleh dimakan. (2) Kotoran hewan yang boleh dimakan. (6) Hewan mati yang tidak memiliki darah mengalir, seperti semut, kecoak, kutu, dan sebagainya. (3) Nanah dari manusia atau dari hewan yang boleh dimakan.

Najis disucikan [atau dihilangkan] dengan mencuci, membasahi, menggosok, atau mengusap, sebagai berikut: Penyucian pakaian yang memiliki najis: Jika najis memiliki substansi atau berat, maka harus digosok dan dikerik kemudian dicuci. Jika najis itu basah atau lembab, maka harus dicuci.

Air kencing bayi laki-laki yang belum makan makanan padat cukup dibasahi saja. Najis di tanah harus diangkat secara fisik kemudian air dituangkan di atas najis cair apa pun. Sepatu disucikan dengan digosok atau berjalan di atas area yang bersih. Barang-barang yang sudah dipoles atau diperhalus, seperti kaca, pisau, ubin, dan barang serupa, dibersihkan dengan diusap. Jika seekor anjing menjilat mangkuk, mangkuk itu harus dicuci tujuh kali, salah satunya dengan tanah.

Etika Beristinja dan Istijmar: Kata al-istinja berarti menghilangkan sisa kotoran atau urine dengan air. Kata al-istijmar berarti menghilangkan sisa kotoran atau urine dengan batu, kertas, atau sejenisnya.

Dianjurkan bagi seseorang untuk masuk ke toilet dengan kaki kiri terlebih dahulu dan membaca doa: "Bismillah, Allahumma inni a'udzu bika minal-khubutsi wal-khaba'its (Dengan nama Allah. Aku berlindung kepada Allah dari setan laki-laki dan perempuan)."

Saat keluar dari toilet, dianjurkan untuk melangkah keluar dengan kaki kanan terlebih dahulu dan membaca: "Ghufranaka, al-hamdu lillahil-ladzi adzhaba 'annil-adza wa 'afani ([Aku memohon] ampunan-Mu. Segala puji bagi Allah yang telah menghilangkan dariku apa yang menyakitkan dan menjadikanku sehat)."

Dianjurkan bagi orang yang buang air kecil untuk bersandar pada kaki kiri [yakni paha kiri]. Juga dianjurkan untuk jauh dari pandangan orang dan menyembunyikan diri. Untuk buang air kecil, sebaiknya memilih tempat yang mencegah urine mengenai pakaiannya.

Dimakruhkan masuk ke toilet dengan membawa sesuatu yang mengandung nama Allah, kecuali ada kebutuhan yang kuat. Juga harus menghindari mengangkat pakaian sebelum dekat dengan tanah. Juga harus menghindari berbicara di toilet. Juga harus menghindari menyentuh kemaluan dengan tangan kanan atau membersihkannya dengan tangan kanan.

Diharamkan menghadap atau membelakangi kiblat [arah shalat] ketika buang hajat di tanah lapang. Jika berada di dalam gedung, hal itu diperbolehkan, meskipun sebaiknya tetap dihindari.

Diharamkan buang air kecil atau besar di jalan-jalan, tempat-tempat teduh yang berguna, di bawah pohon berbuah, dan tempat-tempat serupa lainnya.

Seseorang harus membersihkan dirinya dengan batu bersih, mengusap dirinya tiga kali, jika itu benar-benar membersihkan seseorang. Jika tidak, ia harus mengusap lebih banyak. Dianjurkan untuk menjadikannya bilangan ganjil, tiga, lima, dan seterusnya. Diharamkan membersihkan diri dengan tulang, kotoran, makanan, atau sesuatu yang terhormat. Dibolehkan menghilangkan sisanya dengan air, serbet, atau kertas. Menggabungkan air dan batu lebih baik daripada menggunakan air saja.

Sunan al-Fithrah: Pada dasarnya, ini adalah perbuatan-perbuatan yang sesuai dengan cara alami dan asli manusia diciptakan. Oleh karena itu, inilah perbuatan-perbuatan yang harus dipenuhi manusia dalam kehidupannya. (1) Menggunakan siwak [atau sikat gigi]: Dianjurkan menggunakannya kapan saja, karena ia menyucikan mulut dan menyenangkan Allah. Namun, hal ini lebih ditekankan saat berwudhu untuk shalat, sebelum membaca Al-Qur'an, saat memasuki masjid atau rumah, saat bangun dari tidur, dan kapan pun napas seseorang telah berubah baunya. (2) Mencukur bulu kemaluan, mencabut bulu ketiak, memotong kuku, dan mencuci ruas-ruas jari. (3) Memotong kumis dan membiarkan jenggot tumbuh lebat dan besar. (4) Merawat rambut di kepala dengan cara yang tepat dengan meminyaki dan menyisirnya. Dimakruhkan mencukur sebagian kepala sambil membiarkan sisanya tidak dicukur karena ini dianggap suatu bentuk mutilasi. (5) Mewarnai uban dengan hena atau katam [sejenis tumbuhan yang digunakan untuk mewarnai rambut]. (7) Berkhitan: Ini adalah pemotongan kulup organ kemaluan laki-laki agar kotoran dan urine tidak menumpuk di sana. Untuk perempuan, ini adalah pemotongan ujung klitoris. Khitan adalah perbuatan yang menyucikan dan membersihkan. Khitan merupakan perbuatan yang dianjurkan bagi laki-laki dan perbuatan yang mulia bagi perempuan.

Wudhu adalah penggunaan air yang menyucikan [dalam membasuh] empat anggota badan [dan kepala] dengan cara yang secara khusus ditentukan oleh syariat.

Keutamaan wudhu ditunjukkan dalam hadis Nabi ﷺ yang menyatakan: "Tidaklah salah seorang dari kalian berwudhu dan menyempurnakan wudhunya kemudian mengucapkan, 'Asyhadu an la ilaha illallah wa anna Muhammadan 'abdullahi wa rasuluh,' kecuali dibukakan untuknya delapan pintu surga dan ia dapat masuk dari pintu mana saja yang ia kehendaki." (Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim.)

Menyempurnakan basuhan pada anggota-anggota wudhu, tanpa berlebihan, berhak mendapatkan seseorang di antara al-ghurr al-muhajjalin pada Hari Kebangkitan. Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Umatku akan dipanggil pada Hari Kebangkitan dengan nama ghurr muhajjilin [yang menyiratkan bahwa bagian-bagian tubuh akan bersinar atau bercahaya] karena bekas wudhu. Barangsiapa di antara kalian yang dapat memperpanjang cahayanya hendaklah melakukannya." [Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari dan Muslim.]
content_ar:
e Impure water is purified by it changing its characteristics naturally or by cleaning it or by adding enough pure water to it that the alteration is removed.

© If a المسلمين is in doubt about whether some water is impure or pure, he acts on the basis of what was known for certainty: the general ruling for anything is that of purity [in other words, something is considered pure unless there is clear evidence demonstrating that it has been made impure].

e If one cannot differentiate liquid that is pure from that which is not, one leaves them both and performs tayammum,

e Ifa person is confused about garments, not knowing which is impure or forbidden from that which is pure, he bases his act on what he knows for certain and prays one prayer only [in that garment; in other words, he uses the garment he believes to be pure and prays once; he does not have to pray, change his clothing and then pray another prayer just to make sure that he prayed in a garment that was not impure or forbidden].

The word al-aaniyah (43!) is the plural of inaa (cil). Lexically, it is any container or utensil used for food or drink. The legal definition of the word is the same.

With respect to the composition of utensils, they can be divided into the following categories: (1) utensils made of gold or silver; (2) silver plated utensils; (3) gold plated utensils; (4) expensive utensils due to their material or way of being made; (5) leather utensils; (6) utensils made out of bone; and (7) any other type of utensils, such as those made of pottery or wood.

Every container or utensil made ofa pure substance, costly or not costly, is permissible to be owned and used except for that made from gold or silver or plated with either of them. Hudhaifah narrated that النبي ﷺ (عليه السلام) said,

ioe BUSEY; Sadly SUIT BLAS Y ce BS, GU 3 ad Wh

“Do not drink from a_ gold or silver container and do not eat from such plates for they are for them [the disbelievers] in this world and for us [the believers] in the Hereafter.” (Recorded by al-Bukhari and المسلمين.)

What is forbidden to use is also forbidden to possess in a form that is to be used, like possessing musical instruments which is also forbidden.

Note that the above prohibition applies to both men and women due to the generality of the text.

Nothing is to be considered impure simply based on conjecture until one is certain that it is impure. That is, the basic or general ruling is that of purity.

This includes the utensils of the People of the Book [Jews and Christians] and the utensils of the polytheists. The ruling concerning these utensils is that they are permissible for use as long as one is not certain that they are impure. This is because the general ruling is that of purity.

e The clothing of non-المسلمين is considered pure as long as one is not certain that they contain some impurity.

e The skin of a dead animal from the types of animals that one is normally allowed to eat is purified by tanning.

e What is cut off from a live animal [meaning, flesh or bones] is considered impure like carrion. However, the wool, feathers, hairs and fur are considered pure even if taken from a live animal.

e It is recommended (السنة) to cover the containers and drinking utensils and to close the mouths of waterskins. النبي ﷺ (عليه السلام) said,

the name of الله [over them].” (Recorded by al-Bukhari and المسلمين.)

Lexically, impurities or al-najaasah (4\on3!) means something filthy. Something becomes impure when it is tarnished by something filthy.

In the terminology of Islamic law, impurity refers to a specific amount of particular substances, such as urine, blood and alcohol, whose presence prohibits one from being able to perform the prayer.

There are two types of impurities. One is something that is impure due to its essence and the second is impure with respect to its ruling. The first category includes those articles that are impure in and of themselves such that they cannot be made pure, such as dogs and pigs. The second category covers those things that are impure due to an impure substance coming upon something otherwise pure.

Impurities can be divided into three categories: (1) those substances concerning which there is agreement that they are impure; (2) those substances concerning which there is a disagreement that they are impure; and (3) those substances that are pardoned or overlooked by Islamic law.

(1) Carrion of all land animals [except for locusts]. Dead sea animals are considered pure and permissible to eat.

(2) Blood that has been poured forth, that is, the blood that pours forth from a land animal when it is slaughtered.

(3) Pig flesh [and other parts of the pig].

(7) Al-wadi [an uncommon white substance that flows from the male private part due to a medical condition].

(8) Meat from an animal that one is not allowed to consume.

(9) [Flesh or bones] cut off from a live animal. For example, if one were to cut off the foreleg of a live sheep, that foreleg would be considered impure.

(12) Blood from a prolonged flow from the woman’s private part.

[(13) Al-madhi or prostatic fluid which is a fluid that flows out of the sexual parts due to sexual stimulation.!]

(1) The urine of an animal that one is permitted to eat.

(2) The feces of an animal that one is permitted to eat.

(6) Dead animals that do not have flowing blood, such as ants, cockroaches, fleas and so forth.

(3) Pus from a human or from an animal one is permitted to eat.

' This point was not mentioned in the original text but it is something that is agreed upon—JZ

Impurities are purified [or removed] by washing, moistening, scrubbing or wiping, as follows:

The purification of clothing having impurities: If the impurity has some body or weight to it, it is to be rubbed and scraped off and then washed. Ifthe impurity is wet or moist, then it is to be washed.

The urine of a weaning boy who does not yet eat solid foods needs simply to be moistened.

Impurities on the ground are to be physically removed and then water is poured over any liquid impurity.

Shoes are made pure by rubbing them or walking over pure areas,

Finished or polished items, such as glass, knives, tiles and similar items are cleaned by wiping them.

If a dog licks into a bowl, the bowl is to be washed seven times, one of them being with dirt.

Topic Four: Etiquette and Cleaning Oneself After Relieving Oneself

The word al-istinjaa (¢\~Y\) means to remove the remains of one’s feces or urine with water. The word alistiimaar ()\eo:—~Y|) means to remove the remains of one’s feces or urine with rocks, paper or something of that nature.

It is recommended for the person to enter the bathroom with his left foot first and to say,

or ae, 6 4; y Helly EEN Se all, 5 y5F al ey

“In the name of الله. I seek refuge in الله from the male and female devils.”

Upon leaving the bathroom, it is recommended to step out with the right foot first and to say,

ples oS 2 Caf cil al) Mod sige “(I seek] Your forgiveness. All praise be to الله who removed from me what is harmful and made me healthy.”

It is recommended for the one who is relieving himself to lean on his left leg [meaning the left thigh]. It is also recommended for him to be out of the sight of the people and to conceal himself. For urination, he should choose a place that would prevent his urine from getting on to his clothing.

It is disliked to enter the bathroom with anything containing the name of الله, unless there is some strong need to do so. One should also avoid raising one’s clothing before getting close to the ground. One should also avoid speaking in the bathroom. He should also avoid touching his private part with his right hand or cleaning it with his right hand.

It is forbidden to face or have one’s back toward the qiblah [the direction in which one prays] while relieving oneself in open lands. If one is in a building, it is permissible, although even then it is best to avoid it.

It is forbidden to urinate or defecate in the roadways, useful areas of shade, under a fruit-producing tree and similar other locations [in other words, any place that will bring harm or discomfort to others].

One should clean oneself with a clean rock, wiping oneself three times, if that totally cleans the person. If that does not clean the person, he should wipe himself more. It is recommended to make it an odd number of times, three, five and so forth.

It is forbidden to clean oneself with bones, dung, food or anything respectable. It is allowed to remove the remains

with water, napkins or paper. To combine both water and rocks is better than to use water alone.

It is obligatory to wash with water the parts of one’s clothing that have impurities on them. If one is not sure exactly where such places are, he is to wash the entire garment. It is from the السنة for a man to urinate sitting. It is not disliked for him to do so standing given that he can keep himself free from any impurities landing on him.

Toplc Five: The Acts Corresponding to the Nature of Humans (Sunan al-Fitra

In essence, these are the acts that are consistent with the natural and original way that humans were fashioned to be. Hence, they are the acts that a human should fulfill in his life.

(1) Using the toothstick [or toothbrush]: It is recommend to use this at any time, as it is purifying for the mouth and pleasing to the Lord. However, it is even more emphasized while making abilution for prayer, before reading the القرآن, upon entering the mosque or house, when rising from one’s sleep and whenever one’s breath has changed its smell.

(2) Shaving the pubic hairs, plucking the underarm hairs, trimming the [finger and toe] nails and washing the finger joints and knuckles.

(3) Trimming the moustache and allowing the beard to grow full and big.

(4) Treating the hair on one’s head properly by oiling and combing it. It is disliked to shave part of the head while leaving the rest unshaved. This is considered a type of mutilation.

(5) Dyeing the gray hairs with henna or katam [a type of herb used in dyeing hair].

(7) Getting circumcised: This is the cutting off of the foreskin of the male organ so that filth and urine cannot build up there. With respect to the female, it is the cutting off of the tip of the clitoris, which looks like a seed. [It is not the same as what is known as “female genital mutilation,” which implies the removal of the entire clitoris and which is forbidden.] It is known to those who specialize in this field. Circumcision is an act of purification and cleanliness. It has many advantages to it. It is a recommended act for males and a noble act for females.

Ablution is the use of purifying water [in washing] the four extremities [and the head] in the manner particularly prescribed by the Law.

The virtues of الوضوء are indicated in the الحديث of the Prophet (عليه السلام) which states,

“None of you makes ablution and completes the ablution fully and then says, ‘I bear witness that none is worthy of worship except الله and that Muhammad is the servant of الله and His messenger,’ except that opened for him will be the eight gates of Paradise and he enters from any of them he wishes.” (Recorded by المسلمين.)

Completing the washing fully of the extremities, without extravagance, entitles one to be among al-ghurr almuhajjaloon on the Day of Resurrection. النبي ﷺ (عليه السلام) said,

“My nation will be called on the Day of Resurrection by the name ghurr muhajjileen [implying that the parts of the body will be glittering or radiant] due to the traces of ablution.
